design_philosophy.core_loop: Explore non-linear overworld map, collect medallions and power-ups from governors, defeat bosses to unlock new abilities and areas, manage equipment upgrades via shop currency
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Non-linear progression discovery versus resource scarcity; player must explore strategically to find optimal routes and equipment before facing final boss countdown timer
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Elite operative (Lightning Kid/Big Bang Blitz) infiltrating enemy stronghold; superhero progression fantasy via visible equipment changes and progressive force powers (shrinking, teleportation, flight, healing, invincibility)
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Pioneering open-ended metroidvania design (40+ route possibilities) that respects player agency in exploration order; gate progression through ability acquisition rather than linear keys; finale features guessing-game mechanic (medallion placement puzzle) against countdown timer—embraces consequence and player failure as narrative beats rather than softening difficulty

difficulty.checkpointing: Password-based continue system (test password reveals full max-out capability, suggesting developer-friendly checkpoint philosophy)
difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: High agency in route selection and exploration pacing; player can backtrack to grind currency for upgrades; final sequence forces engagement with consequence (timed medallion defusal with no instructions, working against countdown)

progression.upgrade_system: Shop-based currency system; purchasable weapon upgrades (boomerang gun vs. handgun) and force powers affect both gameplay capability and character visual appearance
progression.unlock_mechanism: Ability-gating: force powers (shrinking, teleportation, flight, healing, invincibility) unlock new areas; six medallions distributed among seven governors serve as progression gates to final confrontation
replayability_hooks: Multiple endings (good vs. bad) based on bomb defusal success/failure
Password system enables save-scumming and different route experimentation
Backtracking and revisiting previously-visited areas encouraged by teleportation ability

world_building_techniques: Briefing cutscene (Commander Hasslewine mobilization orders, removed in US localization)
NPC dialogue encounters (sprite reveals demon trap, hermit teaches force powers, Michael's betrayal reveal)
Environmental narrative: alien technology revelation in final sequence
Meta-narrative fourth wall break: Bang's final dialogue mentions 'making a game based on these adventures' (reflexive commentary on metroidvania design)
Moral themes embedded in finale: aliens judge humanity's destructiveness; Bang's response asserts human self-determination
Bad ending text scroll: thematic statement on scientific progress as double-edged sword, radioactivity, murder weapons, culminating in doomsday bombing

boss_design.philosophy: Seven governors as distributed medallion holders (likely mid-tier encounters); final boss (MadGazer/The Boss—a demon) serves as game's climactic narrative pivot; boss encounters gate ability unlocks and drive story revelation
notable_bosses: Tom Guycot (skeleton mastermind; killed by demon mid-game, creates narrative inversion)
MadGazer/The Boss (demon trapped beneath mountain; mind control antagonist; final confrontation requires Sword of Apollo; represents alien-human conflict resolution)

faction_indicators: S.A.B.R.E. (Special Assault Brigade for Real Emergencies)—player allegiance
Lawbreakers/Governors faction (Japan vs. US localization difference)—antagonist organization
Alien race (thousand-year creators of humanity)—external judgment faction with doomsday capability
danger_signals: Alien disdain for humanity's destructiveness—extinction-level judgment
environmental_storytelling: Overworld map layout reveals seven governor territories (medallion distribution)
Demonhead mountain as geographic focal point (demon imprisoned beneath)
Professor Plum's kidnapping location establishes enemy headquarters geography
Character visual upgrades (equipment changes visible on sprite) telegraph progression status to player
